title: Astellas Pharmaceuticals Enters $1.7 Billion Global Collaboration with Vir Biotechnology to advance PSMA-targeting PRO-XTEN
text: Astellas Pharma and Vir Biotechnology have partnered to develop VIR-5500, an experimental therapy designed to treat advanced prostate cancer. The compound combines PSMA targeting with CD3 T-cell engagement and employs PRO-XTEN masking technology to minimize systemic effects by activating only when reaching tumor sites. Vir receives $335 million upfront, comprising $240 million in cash, a $75 million equity stake purchased at a premium, and a $20 million near-term milestone. Development expenses split 60-40 between Astellas and Vir respectively. The deal includes up to $1.37 billion in future milestones and double-digit royalties on international sales for Vir. Vir continues the ongoing Phase I trial before transitioning leadership to Astellas. Astellas controls global development and holds exclusive commercialization rights outside the U.S., where Vir retains an option to co-promote with equal profit-sharing. Sanofi benefits economically through its prior licensing arrangement with Vir. Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer carries approximately 30% five-year survival rates, creating sustained demand for innovative immunotherapy approaches. The announcement was made on February 23, 2026, and sent Vir shares jumping 36% from $7.56 to $10.29 Tuesday at 9:51 a.m., within the first half hour of trading, before settling down to $9.49 and a 28% gain.
